title: pistolochia rutifolia
intro: Pistolochia rutifolia, also known as the Malabar nut, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Western Ghats of India and Sri Lanka. The Malabar nut has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pistolochia rutifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Pistolochia rutifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
